zh.Calverley, West Riding of Yorkshire, Eng: 英國英格蘭越可爾卡爾福利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter Calverley於1310年出生在英國越可爾卡爾福利時，他的父親John Scott de Calverley, II40歲，母親Joanna Warde23歲。他與Margery Dyneley結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1404年12月18日在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾卡爾福利享年94歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter Calverley于1310年出生在英国越可爾卡爾福利时，他的父亲John Scott de Calverley, II40岁，母亲Joanna Warde23岁。他与Margery Dyneley结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1404年12月18日在英国英格兰韋斯特 越可爾卡爾福利享年94岁。

nameMeanings.name: Calverley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from Calverley in Yorkshire, named with Old English calfra (genitive plural of calf ‘calf’) + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Alternatively, in some instances the name may be a variant of the habitational name Calveley, from a place so called in Cheshire (with the same meaning as above), or from Calverleigh in Devon, meaning ‘the clearing of the bare wood’ (Old English calu ‘bare, bald’ + wudu ‘wood’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’).

variants.name: Calvery
meanings: Possibly an Americanized form of German Kälberer(see Kalberer ).

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Walter Calverley was born in 1310, in Calverley, Yorkshire, England, his father, John Scott de Calverley, II, was 40 and his mother, Joanna Warde, was 23. He married Margery Dyneley. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 18 December 1404, in Calverley, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 94.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter Calverley est né en 1310, à Calverley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Scott de Calverley, II, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Joanna Warde, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Margery Dyneley. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 18 décembre 1404, à Calverley, West Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 94 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Walter Calverley(이)가 1310년 영국 요르크실르 캘벌리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Scott de Calverley, II(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Joanna Warde(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Margery Dyneley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1404년 12월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 캘벌리에서 94세의 나이로 사망했다.
